name: Cathe's Cajun Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1 large Bell pepper, chopped into 1/4" to 1/2" pieces
Green onions (large bunch), cut into 1/4" thick pieces
2 medium Celery stalks, cut lengthwise into 3 sticks, then cut into pieces 1/4" thick
1 lb Shrimp, peeled and de-veined, but not cooked
1/4 t Cayenne pepper flakes (crushed), or more to taste (we use about 1 T)
1 package Pepperidge Farm Cornbread stuffing mix (cellophane bag, brown-labelled)
1 package Pepperidge Farm Herb stuffing mix (blue-labelled)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or Dutch oven (at least 6-quart), cook the sausage over medium heat until lightly-browned, breaking it up into small pieces.
2. Add the vegetables to the pot with the cooked sausage and saute until they begin to brown slightly while the sausage browns more.
3. Add the butter to the pot and stir the pieces around until they are melted.
4. Add the shrimp and cook for 3 or 4 minutes, stirring constantly. The shrimp should turn pink all over and be done, but just barely done. Break one in half and make sure it is opaque in the center.
5. Turn off the heat, but leave the pot on the burner.
6. Add the 2 3/4 cups water, garlic flakes, and cayenne pepper flakes; stir well.
7. Add both bags of stuffing mix to the liquid mixture. Blend all ingredients together well.
8. Lightly spoon the stuffing mixture into a greased 9" x 14" pan.
9. Bake at 350 degrees F for 15 minutes. Serve at once (with Burgundy mushroom gravy, if desired).
